words.mean_en: workable, suitable for real use; likely to work effectively
the act of travelling from one place to another: Did you have a good journey?

1. to move something or go with somebody to another place: Take your coat with you it's cold - Mark took me to the station
2. to put your hand round something and hold it: Take this money it's yours - She took my hand and led me outside
3. to remove something from a place or a person, often without asking them: Somebody has taken my bike
4. to eat or drink something: Don't forget to take your medicine
5. to agree to have something; to accept something: if you take my advice you'll forget all about him
6. to need an amount of time: The journey took four hours - It takes a long time to learn a language
7. to travel in a bus, train, etc: I took a taxi to the hospital

words.mean_fa: چشم‌ها را چرخاندن در واکنش به چیزی احمقانه

کسانی که یک چیز جدید را می‌پذیرند یا شروع به استفاده از آن می‌کنند

words.mean_en:
1. to take something or somebody with you to a place: Could you bring me a glass of water? - Can I bring a friend to the party?
2. to make something happen: Money doesn't always bring happiness

1. in or to a higher place; higher than somebody or something: The sun is above the clouds -> opposite = below
2. more than a number or price: Temperatures above 40°C

1. to give cards to players in a game of cards: Start by dealing seven cards to each player
2. to buy and sell something in business
**deal out** to give something to a number of people: The profits will be dealt out among us
**deal with something** 1. to take action in a particular situation in order to solve a problem or do a particular job: I am too busy to deal with this problem now
2. to be about a special subject: The first chapter of the book deals with letter writing

words.mean_fa: ویراستار نهایی که اشتباهات تایپی، نگارشی و علائم را اصلاح می‌کند

تعجب کردن، با خود فکر کردن

words.mean_en:
1. from a particular time in the past until now: I have been waiting since 8 AM.
2. because; for the reason that: Since you're tired, you should rest.

1. a journey to see a lot of different places: We went on a tour of Scotland
2. a short visit to see a building or city: They gave us a tour of the house

1. happening often or found in many places: jackson is a common English name
2. shared by two or more people or by everybody in a group: they share a common interest in photography

words.mean_fa: تا وقتی که به پایان پارت 1 برسیم

words.mean_en:
1. to try to deal with a difficult problem or situation: How shall we tackle this problem?
2. to try to take the ball from somebody in a game like football
3. to try to catch and hold somebody: The police officer tackled one of the robbers as he ran out

1. to take and hold something that is moving: He threw the ball to me and I caught it
2. to find and hold somebody or something: They caught a fish in the river - The man ran so fast that the police couldn't catch him
3. to see somebody when they are doing something wrong: They caught the thief stealing the painting
4. to be early enough for a bus, train, etc. that is going to leave: You should run if you want to catch the buss (OPPOSITE: miss)
5. to get an illness: She caught a cold
6. to let something be held tightly: I caught my fingers in the door
**catch fire** to start to burn: The house caught fire
to have something in your hand or arms; to keep someone in a position
a person who steals money or property, often by force

allowing direct communication between user and program
additional, more than is usual or needed
large in amount, degree, or intensity
nearly, not completely but very close
not complete; only a part of something
more than what is necessary or normal
able, having the ability to do something
to make a small change to something, to make it better: You can adjust the height of this chair
to talk or write about a topic in detail
guidelines, information telling you how to do something
usually, in most cases; as expected
far from one side to the other: We drove down a wide road
able to exist or work together without problems
to like one thing or person better than another: I would prefer to stay at home
an official instruction about what to do or not to do
the place where an event or meeting happens

1. to stay in the same way; to not change: I asked her a question but she remained silent.
2. to stay after other people or things have gone: After the fire, very little remained of the house.

1. if something is voluntary, you do it because you want to, not because you must: She made a voluntary decision to leave the job.

to move away from the correct path or subject

based on feelings rather than facts
used to show two different things or people that you can choose (examples: You can have either tea or coffee. / I will either write or telephone.)
related to the mind or thinking
the ability to understand something without conscious reasoning

1. to manage or control somebody or something: A policeman was in the middle of the road, directing the traffic.
2. to be in charge of actors in a play or a film: The movie was directed by Quentin Tarantino
3. to tell somebody how to get to a place: Can you direct me to the station, please?
a list of items (e.g., books, products) with details
very good: She speaks excellent Spanish

1. to pay money for something: Louise spends a lot of money on clothes
2. to pass time: I spent the summer in Italy
a job that you learn to do and then do for many years
function or part played by a person in a particular situation
the special work that a person or thing does: the function of the heart is to send blood round the body
the things that are happening in a certain place or at a certain time: We are in a difficult situation at the moment

words.mean_fa: در محدوده … بودن، شامل شدن از … تا

words.mean_en: a new plan, project or business

1. the work of making things in factories: Is there much industry in your country?
2. all the companies that make the same thing: Japan has a big car industry
a particular area of knowledge or skill that someone focuses on

1. use / put into practice: Apply a theme to your phone
2. request formally: Mahdi applied for a job in Germany

1. a thing that you put over another thing, for example to keep it safe: The computer has a plastic cover
2. the outside part of a book or magazine: The book had a picture of a film star on the cover (= the front cover)

1. one of the symbols (0-9) that we use to show numbers: Shall I write the numbers in words or figures?
2. an amount or price: What are our sales figures for Spain this year?
3. the shape of a person's body: She's got a good figure
4. a shape of a person that you cannot see clearly: I saw a tall figure outside the window
clearly, brightly - in a way that produces strong, clear images in the mind
far from other people or things: an Isolated house in the mountains
a form of a language spoken in a particular area: a local dialect

1. a type of something: Cars, trains and buses are all forms of transport
2. a piece of paper with spaces for you to answer questions: You need to fill in this form to get a new passport
3. the shape of a person or thing: a cake in the form of a car
4. one of the ways you write or say a word: 'Forgot' is a form of 'forget'
land outside towns and cities, with fields, forests, etc.

1. a piece of land used for animals or for growing crops, usually surrounded by a fence, trees, etc
2. an area of study or knowledge: Dr Smith is an expert in her field
3. a piece of land used for something special: a sports field - an airfield (= a place where planes land and take off)
all the plants of one kind that a farmer grows at one time: There was a good crop of potatoes last year - Rain is good for the crops

1. the buying and selling of things: trade between Britain and the US
2. a particular type of business: the building trade
3. a job for which you need special skills, especially with your hands: Dave is a plumber by trade - to learn a trade

1. a place where trains stop so that people can get on and off
2. a place where buses or coaches start and end their journeys: the bus station
3. a building for some special work: the police station - the fire station - a petrol station
4. a television or radio company
5. a person's social rank or position: a person of high station
to lose brightness, color, or strength gradually

1. to or in another place: She ran away from him - He put his book away
2. from a place: The sea is two miles away
3. not here: Tim is away from school today because he is ill
4. in the future: Our holiday is only three weeks away

to make someone or something different from others: His kindness sets him apart from others
people or animals that live in a particular place
the large landmasses on Earth (e.g., Asia, Africa, Europe, etc.)
diverse, consisting of many different kinds

1. without any doubt: She is certainly the best swimmer in the team
2. used when answering questions to mean 'of course': 'Will you open the door for me, please?' 'Certainly' - 'Are you going to apologize?' 'Certainly not!'

words.mean_fa: سریع، با سرعت بالا (زندگی یا تکنولوژی)

words.mean_en: to do something to show that you are happy for a special reason or because it is a special day: If you pass your exams, we'll have a party to celebrate - Grandma celebrated her 90th birthday last week
the ability to create new things or think of new ideas
the ability to take action independently, without being told what to do

1. to get something that you want or need: I gained useful experience from that job
2. to get more of something: I have gained weight recently
to find a solution to a problem or understand how something works

1. to show or to make a connection between two or more things: I found it difficult to relate the two ideas in my mind
2. relate to somebody or something to be connected to somebody or something: We don't need to listen to this, as it doesn't relate to our situation

1. to let somebody have something: She gave me a watch for my birthday - I gave my ticket to the man at the door - Give the letter to your mother when you've read it
2. to make a sound or movement: Jo have me an angry look - He gave a shout - She gave him a kiss
3. to make somebody have or feel something: That noise is giving me a headache - whatever gave you that idea?

1. a possibility that something may happen: There's no chance that she'll come now - She has a good chance of becoming team captain - He doesn't stand (= have) a chance of passing the exam
2. a time when you can do something (same meaning : opportunity): it was their last chance to escape - Be quiet and give her a chance to explain
3. when something happens that you cannot control or that you have not planned (same meaning: luck): We must plan this carefully. I don't want to leave anything to chance - We met by chance at the station

1. very bad: That was a serious mistake
2. important: a serious decision
3. not funny: a serious film
4. if you are serious, you are not joking or playing: Are you serious about going to live in Spain? - You look very serious. Is something wrong?

over, 1. from one side to the other side of something: We walked across the field - A smile spread across her face - The river was about twenty metres across
2. on the other side of something: There is a bank just across the road

1. a method or style of doing something: What is the best way to learn a language?
2. a road or path that you must follow to go to a place: Can you tell me the way to the station please? - I lost my way and I had to look at the map
3. a direction; where somebody or something is going or looking: Come this way - She was looking the other way - is this picture the right way up? - Those two words are the wrong way round
4. distance: It's a long way from Glasgow to London
a particular area of activity, knowledge, or data
to show or make known something that was hidden or unknown

words.mean_fa: شکافتن، ترک خوردن؛ در معنای مجازی: حل کردن (مسئله دشوار)، رمزگشایی کردن

words.mean_en: a lot: I don't like him very much

1. very near (SAME MEANING: beside): The telephone is by the door - They live by the sea
2. a word that shows who or what did something: a painting by Matisse - She was caught by the police
3. using or doing something: I go to work by train - He paid by cheque - You turn the computer on by pressing this button
4. as a result of something: I got on the wrong bus by mistake - We met by chance
5. not later than: I must finish this work by six o'clock
6. from one side of somebody or something to the other (SAME MEANING: past): He walked by me without speaking
7. used for showing the measurements of an area: The table is six feet by three feet (= six feet long and three feet wide)
